growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Nitrate mineral salts (NMS) medium, standard (1 uM CoCl2)

up.method: gas chromatography (GC-FID); headspace CH4 quantified as % atmospheric composition vs known standards and converted to mass using headspace volume and CH4 specific gravity 0.622 mg/ml at 37C

sec.method: not quantified; CO2 named as dissimilation end-product and RuBisCO/CO2 re-assimilation shown essential, but no secretion rate reported

yields.Yxs: 0.48 g dry cell weight (DCW) per g CH4
yields.yield_note: Biomass yield on methane in standard NMS (1 uM CoCl2) at 48 h; experimentally measured (biomass from OD600->DCW, CH4 consumed by GC); P=0.006 vs optimized medium

growth_records.cite: Henard, Jessica M. et al. CRISPRi-based functional genomic screening identifies genes essential for CH4-dependent growth in a methanotrophic bacterium. Science Advances (2026). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.aed4234. [PMC13108547]
growth_records.method: not reported numerically (high-resolution growth curves via CGQ optical sensors / OD600 shown only graphically in Fig. 6B-C; no mu or td value stated in text)
growth_records.snippet: M. capsulatus exhibited a 1.4-fold improvement in biomass yield within 48 hours in NMS medium with 0.01 uM CoCl2 compared to standard NMS [Fig. 6C, 0.65 versus 0.48 g dry cell weight (DCW)/g CH4, P = 0.006]
growth_records.notes: Experimental biomass yield on CH4 = 0.48 g DCW/g CH4 for STANDARD NMS (1 uM CoCl2), measured over 48 h in batch serum vials with 20% CH4 in air at 37C. CH4 consumption measured directly by GC-FID (converted to mass via specific gravity 0.622 mg/ml at 37C); biomass via OD600 with empirical conversion OD600=1 equals 0.254 +/- 0.0249 g DCW/liter. No specific growth rate (mu) or doubling time reported numerically in text (growth curves only graphical). CH4 uptake was measured (Fig. 6C solid lines) but the numeric qCH4 rate is not given in the text/tables, only plotted; hence uptake rate left null. Full NMS composition not stated in this paper (cited as previously described, ref 45); only CoCl2 concentration (1 uM standard) is explicitly given, so medium_composition_stated=false. n=6 to 10, two independent experiments.

growth_records.medium: Nitrate mineral salts (NMS) medium, Co-optimized (0.01 uM CoCl2)

up.method: gas chromatography (GC-FID); headspace CH4 quantified as % atmospheric composition vs standards, converted to mass via CH4 specific gravity 0.622 mg/ml at 37C

yields.Yxs: 0.65 g dry cell weight (DCW) per g CH4
yields.yield_note: Biomass yield on methane in Co-optimized NMS (0.01 uM CoCl2) at 48 h; 1.4-fold higher than standard NMS (0.65 vs 0.48 g DCW/g CH4, P=0.006); improved growth kinetics equate to 4.7-fold higher biomass productivity (g/L/h) vs standard (Fig. 6D, exact productivity value only in figure)

growth_records.cite: Henard, Jessica M. et al. CRISPRi-based functional genomic screening identifies genes essential for CH4-dependent growth in a methanotrophic bacterium. Science Advances (2026). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.aed4234. [PMC13108547]
growth_records.method: not reported numerically (high-resolution growth curves via CGQ/OD600 in Fig. 6B-C; reduced lag phase and 4.7-fold higher biomass productivity noted, but no mu/td value stated in text)
growth_records.snippet: M. capsulatus exhibited a 1.4-fold improvement in biomass yield within 48 hours in NMS medium with 0.01 uM CoCl2 compared to standard NMS [0.65 versus 0.48 g DCW/g CH4, P = 0.006] ... equates to a 4.7-fold improvement in methanotroph biomas
growth_records.notes: Experimental biomass yield on CH4 = 0.65 g DCW/g CH4 for Co-OPTIMIZED NMS (0.01 uM CoCl2), 48 h batch, 20% CH4 in air, 37C. 1.4-fold improvement over standard NMS (0.65/0.48=1.35, reported as 1.4-fold; P=0.006). 4.7-fold improvement in biomass productivity (g DCW/L/h) over standard NMS driven mainly by reduced lag phase; the absolute productivity number is shown only in Fig. 6D and is not given numerically in text. CH4 consumption measured by GC (Fig. 6C) but numeric qCH4 not tabulated -> uptake rate null. Biomass via OD600 conversion OD600=1 equals 0.254 g DCW/liter. Full NMS recipe cited from ref 45, not restated; only CoCl2 (0.01 uM) explicit -> medium_composition_stated=false. n=6 to 10, two independent experiments.
